title: Module 4 Lesson 5 Overview
description: A knowledge check overview containing 2 question(s) to test your understanding of the lesson.

questions.question:
Trial & Iterate

In the four-step selection framework, why is 'Trial & Iterate' the final step rather than 'Choose the Perfect Platform'?

options.label: Because no platform is perfect so expectations should be lowered.
Because platforms frequently shut down so users need backup options.
Because it's impossible to know what someone needs without trying everything.
Because what serves someone changes over time, and trial reveals fit better than analysis alone.

synthesis.q1_a: This is too pessimistic. The point is that the right platform for someone evolves as their practice deepens.
synthesis.q1_b: Stability is a factor, but not the core reason for iteration in facilitation.
synthesis.q1_c: Trial & Iterate doesn't mean random testing; it means informed starting choices followed by refinement.
synthesis.q1_d: Correct! Beginners outgrow beginner platforms, and clinical needs shift as healing progresses. The right answer now isn't the right answer forever.

questions.question:
Assessing Readiness

A client says. 'I want the most powerful VR consciousness experience available - whatever produces the deepest altered states.' What's your most important first response?

options.label: Ask about their hoped-for outcome and support systems.
Discuss privacy concerns vs. content access.

synthesis.q2_a: Budget matters eventually, but safety and readiness come first.
synthesis.q2_b: Tech skills are practical, but psychological readiness for intensity is more critical.
synthesis.q2_c: Correct! This explores the underlying need (what are they seeking?) and assesses readiness (do they have preparation and support?). High-intensity work requires a container, not just an app.
synthesis.q2_d: Privacy is important, but secondary to the safety implications of altered-state requests.
